searchDocs.title: Connection​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Connection { Disconnect: () → () }

searchDocs.title: Event<T>​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Event<T> { Connect: (T) → Connection Once: (T) → Connection }

searchDocs.title: Component<T>​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Component<T> { Value: T ComponentChanged: Event<T> } ComponentChanged is read only, Value can be written to. ECS.CreateComponent("Test", 100) ECS.NewEntity() local component = ECS.GiveComponent(1, "Test") component.ComponentChanged:Connect(function(newValue) print(newValue) -- Prints 300 end) component.Value = 300 You are able to get intellisense by casting the type. ECS.CreateComponent("Intellisense", true) ECS.NewEntity() local component = ECS.GiveComponent(1, "Intellisense") :: ECS.Component<boolean> component.Value = false -- Valid component.Value = 100 -- Type error You will get an error if you attempt to run the following code. ECS.CreateComponent("Errors", 0) ECS.NewEntity() local component = ECS.GiveComponent(1, "Errors") component.ComponentChanged = "foo" -- Error component.NewKey = "bar" -- Error print(component.RealValue) -- Error

searchDocs.title: Entity<T>​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Entity<T> { Components: number Instance: T? ComponentAdded: Event<string> ComponentRemoved: Event<string> } An entity table, ComponentAdded and ComponentRemoved return the name of the component that was removed when calling functions connected to them. All of these values are read only.

searchDocs.title: _ENTITY_TAG_NAME​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> ECS._ENTITY_TAG_NAME: string Name of the CollectionService tag given to instances bound to an entity.

searchDocs.title: _ENTITY_ID_STRING​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> ECS._ENTITY_ID_STRING: string Name of the attribute given to instances bound to an entity. The attribute holds the ID of the entity.

searchDocs.title: QueryWithout​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.QueryWithout( withoutList: {string},-- Components it must not have ...: string-- Names of components ) → ( number,-- ID of Entity ...any-- Value it has with the given components ) Used in for loops. Returns the ID and Value they have with the given components in the second argument, if they contain a component that is in the given array, then they are skipped over and not returned.

searchDocs.title: QueryWith​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.QueryWith( withList: {string},-- Components it must have ...: string-- Names of components ) → ( number,-- ID of Entity ...any-- Value it has with the given components ) Used in for loops. Returns the ID and Value the entity has with the given components in the second argument. Entities must have the component given in the first argument.

searchDocs.title: Query​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.Query( ...: string-- Names of components ) → ( number,-- ID of Entity ...any-- Value it has with the given components ) Used in for loops. Returns the ID and the value it currently has with the given components. ECS.CreateComponent("Test1", 100) ECS.CreateComponent("Test2", true) ECS.NewEntity() ECS.NewEntity() local id = ECS.NewEntity() ECS.GiveComponent(id, "Test1") for i, v in ECS.Query("Test1") print(i, v) -- 3 100 -- Only the third entity is looped over end

searchDocs.title: NewEntity​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.NewEntity(instance: Instance? ) → ( number,-- The ID of the Entity Entity<Instance >-- The Entity table ) Creates a new entity and returns the ID and Entity type, optional instance to bind it to.

searchDocs.title: BulkNewEntities​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.BulkNewEntities( n: number?,-- Defaults to 1 instanceArray: {Instance }?,-- Priority over n ...: string?-- Components to be given upon creation ) → {number}-- List of IDs of the entities created Creates multiple entities at once, if instanceArray is nil, n is used, if both are nil, only one entity is created and returned, ... is the names of the components that are given when creating the entities.

searchDocs.title: BindToInstance​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.BindToInstance( id: number,-- Entity ID instance: Instance? -- Instance to bind to, nil if unbinding ) → () Binds an entity to an instance, if no instance is provided then the current instance binded to the entity is unbinded from it.

searchDocs.title: GetEntity​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.GetEntity( id: number-- ID of Entity ) → Entity<Instance > Returns the Entity type

searchDocs.title: RemoveEntity​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.RemoveEntity( id: number-- ID of Entity ) → () Removes an entity

searchDocs.title: ClearAllEntities​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.ClearAllEntities() → () Removes all the existing entities

searchDocs.title: CreateComponent​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.CreateComponent( name: string,-- Name of component value: T-- Default value for the component ) → () Creates a new component with value T, the value given to it will be the default that is assigned to entities whenever they are given the component. ECS.CreateComponent("Test", 1000) -- 1000 is the default value ECS.NewEntity() local component = ECS.GiveComponent(1, "Test") print(component.Value) -- 1000 Invalid Values table, thread, userdata, and function will cause the function to error, these types are not allowed to be used for the value of the component.

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"Invalid value"	Value given is an invalid value "Exceeded maximum"	There is a maximum of 32 components that can be created

searchDocs.title: DestroyComponent​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.DestroyComponent( name: string-- Name of component ) → () Destroys the component, entities with this component will have it removed, frees up space for the creation of another component.

searchDocs.title: RemoveComponent​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.RemoveComponent( id: number,-- Remove component from an entity name: string-- Name of component to remove ) → () Removes a component from a given entity.

searchDocs.title: GiveComponent​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.GiveComponent( id: number,-- ID of Entity name: string-- Name of component to give ) → Component<T> Gives the component to the given entity, returns the component

searchDocs.title: HasComponent​
searchDocs.content: </> ECS.HasComponent( id: number,-- ID of entity name: string-- Name of component ) → boolean Checks if the given entity has the specified component.
